name: Charlie Watts
abstract: Charles Robert Watts (2 June 1941 – 24 August 2021) was an English musician who was the drummer of the Rolling Stones from 1963 until his death in 2021. Originally trained as a graphic artist, Watts developed an interest in jazz at a young age and joined the band Blues Incorporated.

works.t: I Just Want to Make Love to You
I Just Want to Make Love to You

about_quotes.speaker: Tim Ries
about_quotes.evidence: often I would set up a gig and he would come and play with me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tim Ries interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, March 19, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Keith Ingham
about_quotes.evidence: Charlie Watts, the drummer with them, I knew him in London. He used to come in and sit in with the band where we played
about_quotes.doc_title: Keith Ingham interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Mike Davis
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Do you remember that quote from Charlie Watts about you? [INTERVIEWEE] I do, yeah.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mike Davis interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 1, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Ascione
about_quotes.evidence: even Charlie Watts now he’s playing jazz because he made a trillion dollars with the Stones
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Ascione interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 17, 2001
